topic: A college student discovers their roommate has been reading their journal
raw_output:
[story 1]

The rhythmic tapping of my pen against the desk was becoming a grating soundtrack to my existence. I slammed the notebook shut, the sound echoing sharply in the otherwise quiet dorm room. My eyes darted toward the corner where Liam was sprawled on his bed, a book resting open on his chest. A flicker of something hot and prickly rose in my chest. I stalked over, my steps heavy, and peered down. His gaze was fixed on the open pages, his expression unreadable. "What are you reading?" I demanded, my voice sharper than I intended. He looked up slowly, a slight, almost apologetic shrug accompanying his glance. "Just catching up," he mumbled, not closing the book. The air between us suddenly felt thick, charged with unspoken tension. I couldn't stand the waiting anymore.

[story 2]

I paced the narrow space between my desk and the window, my foot repeatedly striking the worn carpet. Every minute stretched into an agonizing expanse. I watched Liam from across the room, his silhouette framed against the afternoon light, his attention completely absorbed by the small, leather-bound book lying on his lap. A low, frustrated sound escaped my throat. I stopped abruptly, planting my hands on my hips, the tension radiating off me like heat. "Seriously, Liam? Can you just put that down for a second?" I asked, my tone laced with an edge I couldn't quite suppress. He blinked, startled out of his reverie, and quickly tried to cover the pages, a flush creeping up his neck. "I was just looking," he said, his voice a little too quick. The need to know, to have that private space respected, was becoming unbearable.

[story 3]

My jaw was clenched so tightly I could feel the muscles throbbing in my cheeks. I kept glancing toward Liam’s side of the room, the silence punctuated only by the frantic drumming of my own pulse. It was ridiculous; he was just reading. But the sheer duration of it, the quiet consumption of my thoughts, was pushing me to the brink. I walked over, not with aggression, but with a stiff, deliberate stride. I reached out, my fingers hovering near the book, before finally snatching it gently from his hands. His eyes widened, a flash of genuine surprise crossing his face. "Hey," I managed, my voice surprisingly steady despite the internal pressure building behind my eyes. "What is this?"
